content:
Lex Fridman speaks with Jean-Baptiste Kempf and Kieran Kunhya, the lead engineers behind the widely used open-source projects VLC and FFmpeg. They discuss the culture, impact, and ethics behind building foundational software that runs on billions of devices, from everyday gadgets to space missions.

• Open source thrives on meritocracy—contributors from teenagers to engineers in war zones are judged solely on code quality, not background or credentials.  
• FFmpeg has interplanetary scaling, running on billions of devices and even enabling image compression on NASA’s Mars 2020 rover.  
• Contributing to these projects offers elite, real-world programming training through rigorous code reviews by expert low-level developers.  
• Integrity is paramount—JB refused millions to keep VLC ad-free and spyware-free, ensuring its reputation as a trusted, ethical software worldwide.
